name: Code Search Results Panel
description: Editor-tab WebviewPanel that shows results of the hybrid code search (search-code quick command) with readable code snippets — replacing the old QuickPick, which could only show one detail line per result. The palette command (codeWorkbench.searchCode) still prompts for a query via input box, then opens/reveals a single reused panel (searchPanel.ts showSearchPanel) rendering the shared @code-workbench/ui SearchPanel React component (webview entry: webview/search.tsx). RPC: `search(query)` runs scanHost.searchCode (limit 50), widens each result's ~4-line ranking snippet to up to 12 real lines by re-reading the file, and retitles the tab "Search: <query>"; `openFile(file, line)` opens the file at the symbol's line. The panel has its own query bar (Enter re-searches without re-running the command); re-invoking the command pushes a `run-search` event into the existing panel.
guidelines: Snippet widening is best-effort — on file read failure keep the ranking snippet from code-search.mjs.
Single panel instance: reveal + postEvent('run-search') instead of creating a second tab.
UI stays host-agnostic: SearchPanel talks only through SearchApi (search/openFile); the extension wires it via webviewRpc/bridge like the other panels.
Style with --vscode-* theme variables (like ArchPanel), not the clay design tokens — this is a full editor tab, it should match the user's theme.
